overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: no questionnaire or acknowledgment may waive state franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains all rights under California Franchise Investment Law and Relations Act regardless of what the FA says

overrides.override_summary: CA Business and Professions Code §20000-20043 provides franchisee rights concerning termination, transfer, and non-renewal that supersede inconsistent FA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: State law provides additional protections beyond the FA terms

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy is listed as a non-curable default in the FA but may be unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs franchises located in Illinois, superseding the FA's California choice of law
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law rather than CA law applies to IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction or venue outside Illinois is void (except arbitration)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in California; arbitration in CA still permitted

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: no questionnaire or acknowledgment may waive state franchise law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects IL franchisee rights under IL Franchise Disclosure Act

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not apply to any liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Renewal and transfer release requirements cannot waive MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Same as CA — bankruptcy default may be unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring lawsuit in Maryland for MD Franchise Law claims; 3-year limitations period
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can sue in their home state for franchise law violations

overrides.override_summary: Litigation outside Minnesota prohibited; no jury trial waiver, no liquidated damages, no termination penalties, no judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Comprehensive protection: MN franchisees cannot be forced to litigate outside MN

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (60 days to cure); 180 days' notice of non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Much longer notice periods than the FA's 5/30-day cure periods

overrides.override_summary: General release prohibited
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees cannot be required to sign a general release for renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must protect franchisee's rights to use trademarks or indemnify franchisee against trademark claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Affirmative obligation on franchisor to protect MN franchisee trademark rights

overrides.override_summary: NY General Business Law rights (GBL §687.4, §687.5) preserved for renewal and transfer; release/waiver cannot eliminate these rights
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees retain all GBL Article 33 rights regardless of release language

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law (replaces 60-day notice requirement)
overrides.why_it_matters: Broader termination right for NY franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Choice of California law does not waive any right under NY GBL Article 33
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise protections apply regardless of CA choice of law

overrides.override_summary: General release not required of ND franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be forced to sign a release for renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration must be at location agreeable to parties, not remote from franchisee's business; if no agreement, AAA rules determine location
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees not forced to arbitrate in Carlsbad, CA

overrides.override_summary: If conflict between CA law and ND law, ND law prevails
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law supersedes CA choice of law for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Any provision restricting jurisdiction/venue to forum outside RI or requiring laws of another state is void under RI Franchise Investment Act §19-28.1-14
overrides.why_it_matters: CA venue and CA choice of law are void for RI franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: Some non-curable defaults in FA may not constitute 'reasonable cause' under VA law

overrides.override_summary: Anti-waiver: no questionnaire or acknowledgment may waive state franchise law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: VA franchisee rights preserved regardless of FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in event of conflict with FA
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law supersedes CA choice of law for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location; litigation for WA Act claims may be brought in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees not forced to arbitrate in Carlsbad, CA

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver excludes WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights; only valid if negotiated after FA in effect with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-hoc releases with independent counsel required; blanket releases void

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/yr and independent contractors <$250K/yr (RCW 49.62.020/030). No-poach provisions void (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: Although Jazzercise has no noncompete, WA adds explicit statutory voiding plus no-poach protections

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law (Ch. 135) supersedes any conflicting FA provision
overrides.why_it_matters: WI Fair Dealership Law provides termination, renewal, and transfer protections that override the FA
